feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /int/ International: Something i've learned from Anglo-Saxon tourists is they think the whole world s…
entry.content: Something i've learned from Anglo-Saxon tourists is they think the whole world speak english or should speak english. They can't comprehend people not speaking it. They frown and leave once they see you don't speak it, like they're genuinely upset from it. So now i always pretend not to speak english so i don't have to interact with them in town, and it's funny to see them mad. I want every angloid to have a shit time here. Get the fuck out of Nice you pale ass fat fucking poof.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: This game is fun as fuck what the fuck???
entry.content: Why do people hate Dirge of Cerberus like crazy? The gameplay is alright, the story is fine, it's not any more whacky or nonsensical or tranime than actual FF 7. So why is it so hated? My only complain is that it's like 8 hours long. It would have been perfect if it was 4 or 6. 8 is too much.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: /tv/ zeitgeist
entry.content: Post any 4 sequential images from your phone's /tv/ image bank within the last 30 days. This is for a research project to understand better shitposting dynamics. Go. It will be fun.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: 3x3 - 10x10 Thread (validate my taste ... plz)
entry.content: Another one of these, but talk about the games you love or perhaps hate. You can even make a 3x3 for games you dislike instead this time around. I've been playing the Xenia version of WET lately and it emulates at a solid 80-90 FPS, it's a fuckin' dream. Been also grinding through the other GTAs, finished 1 and 2, finished 3, now I'm starting Vice City, with San Andreas soon to be next. 2 is the best IMO, but 3 has it's charm and moments. Claude Speed FTW. https://topsters.org/

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: I literally do not care about permanent ownership of games. I think of them like…
entry.content: I literally do not care about permanent ownership of games. I think of them like food or a vacation experience, I consume them then move on with my life, almost never replay them. Why is everyone having a meltdown over this shit?

She opens a hotel in hopes that patients will be "checking out" into Heaven. Hazbin Hotel Pilot: https://www.you

entry.title: /o/ Auto: Been driving a 2000 Mazda Protege since I was 18. I've decided to replace my veh…
entry.content: Been driving a 2000 Mazda Protege since I was 18. I've decided to replace my vehicle now since it's 26 years old. Wanting to get the 2026 Prius, any thoughts on it? I won't buy a used car, I'm treating myself to a new vehicle after driving this old beater for so long.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Making use of LLMs
entry.content: I'm developing lore for a fiction thing, doesn't really matter what. It's about the most flexible, error-tolerant endeavor you can imagine. Mistakes into miracles-type nonsense, almost. And yet, I am struggling to apply LLMs to the task in any productive way. For example, I write up some new facts and make it rework documents from the project files, so the old ones can be replaced with updated ones. I go >okay, there's cyclops in the mountains. They got one eye each and are big. the LLM will cra

entry.title: /b/ Random: So I'm pretty sure E is actually Funnyjunk's Admin. Think about it, Admin is abs…
entry.content: So I'm pretty sure E is actually Funnyjunk's Admin. Think about it, Admin is absolutely terrified of turds and barf, including the glorious logs King Andy produces daily, and will viciously persecute any who post them on his site. And on 4chan, where no such persecution exists, E is trying to convince the mods to institute similar authoritarian measures. So they're one and the same. What say you, fellow logposters?

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Why hasn't LaTeX been replaced with something better yet?
entry.content: >install 7 GB of tooling to write a paragraph >compiler error points to line 7, problem is on line 412: missing one brace >undefined control sequence, refuses to elaborate >package conflict discovered only after 20-minute compile >overfull \hbox >spend longer aligning a figure than doing the research >lualatex, xelatex, pdflatex literally wtf https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KhW wuQ7qf5A

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Best way yo learn Javascript?
entry.content: The current tutorials I am watching take at least 10 minutes to explain obviously stuff like const x = document.getElementById('x'); x.textcontent = whatever; like basic shit and it feels like im wasting my time, is there a better way or should I just cope?

entry.title: /trash/ Off-Topic: Artist/Commissioner Rant General #1037
entry.content: /acrg/ - Artist/Commissioner Rant General #1037 Nasty cock orifice Edition Remember to SCREENCAP your links so we can laugh when they delete their posts. Talk about artists or commissioners, mostly the bad ones or those who got under your skin. Share your experiences and get angry with us. For all artists (and commissioners who are close friends with artists) here, beware of: Horu, Pixxy, OkiOppai, Timedwatcher, AnythingGoes, Skitterleaf, kathielnotfound (Jocelyn Jones), and Snoopcrow/jbunny/j6u

entry.title: /g/ Technology: What is causing this mental illness?
entry.content: People are unironically dedicating hundreds of hours studying parsing for compilers, in order to create a programming language syntax which is inherently inferior because the underlying theory is designed to significantly reduce its potential to be easily composable, they're opting for a solution which forces a linear sequential dependency between adjacent terms and which imposes an unnecessary binding between its semantics and the english language which is inherently ambiguous.

entry.title: /news/ News: Liggerals still sleeping, so I'll do it.
entry.content: Demonstrators in white supremacist attire protest on Capitol Hill https://www.politico.com/news/2026/ 07/04/patriot-front-white-supremaci st-protests-july-4-00987020 Demonstrators donning the logo and insignia of Patriot Front, a white supremacist group, were seen protesting in the Eastern Market neighborhood and on Capitol Hill on the 250th anniversary of U.S. independence. Sporting white masks, sunglasses and Patriot Front’s signature tan caps, the protesters carried Confederate, Patriot Front

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Hello /mu/, looking for some information on the music industry and whatever else…
entry.content: Hello /mu/, looking for some information on the music industry and whatever else. I’ve been making music since I was young, and a lot of it has been used by other artists branded as their work. To quickly try and explain my situation: it’s like Harkness from Fallout 3, but instead of him doing it to hide from the people chasing him down, my identity and memory was changed to stop me from doing what I usually do, which to put into perspective, is like the game Getting Up. So my question is, what’

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: After the dust has settled, can we admit that it was kino?
entry.content: After the dust has settled, can we admit that it was kino?

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: BUCK GUNN: Directors Now Avoiding DCU Work
entry.content: >DC’s biggest problem coming out of the Supergirl disaster isn’t the write-down. It’s the question underneath the entire slate: who is going to make these movies? >Word around town, per our Hollywood insiders, is that filmmakers are hesitant to take on big DC franchises. And that hesitancy has only hardened since Supergirl bombed, for two reasons our insiders spell out. >We Reported The DCU Director Problem Back In October >First, the timestamp. This isn’t post-flop pile-on. >Back in October, ou

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Bros, I think Half-Life 3’s been cancelled.
entry.content: The game simply doesn’t exist. Unlike GTA 6, which actually took 13 years and US$2 billion to develop, Half-Life 3 has simply been cancelled. The game doesn’t exist at all. No matter how much we pray to the god Gayben, a miracle won’t happen. Just give up.

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: I'm too young to have a natural death right now
entry.content: It's not something I like. Some soul said," you might get reincarnated as a blond and blue-eyed prince but you are doomed to live your whole life as a demon lord's possession if you die by killing yourself." I'm afraid, I don't want to be a possession, I want to be free. So, will I have to wait for my death?

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: 'Hancock' original script, then called 'Tonight, He Comes'
entry.content: >John Hancock is a nihilistic, depressed, alcoholic superhuman who lives in New York City. Due to his powers, Hancock believes that it's his obligation to protect the innocent, even though he hates having the weight of the world in his shoulders, and attempts to dull the pain by indulging in drugs and prostitutes. >After foiling a robbery, Hancock befriends bank teller Mary Longfellow, who is estranged from her husband Horus, a cowardly security guard. Horus and Mary's sex life is dull, and Horu

entry.title: /g/ Technology: A man named Thom Henderson wrote a compression program called ARC...
entry.content: ... back in the 1980s. He distributed his code widely as shareware, along with source - because how else would people be able to use it and adjust it for their machines? - but directly said he "did it for the mail". Then a guy named Phil Katz (yes, he is) took the source code, threw assembly into the speed-critical parts (thus tying it to x86) and released it as paid software (thus violating ARC's license) called PKARC. Henderson heard about it, and called in independent software expert John Nav

Used to like learning about functional programming and Lisp, make cool abstractions that improve codebase maintainability, learn about best practices and actually solving problems the right way and whatnot. Now after 5 months of the "AI special" in an undisclosed company, I can barely even explain what the code I'm "writing" does anymore, the codebase is fucking slop and I'm never reading t

entry.title: /g/ Technology: >use perplexity to find good cdn for global userscript I'm working on >gives me …
entry.content: >use perplexity to find good cdn for global userscript I'm working on >gives me a link to maxcdn >company shut down years ago >was once a valid link >domain was purchased by someone else and the cdn urls were replaced with obfuscated cookie stealer >have to change all of my passwords to reset cookies I think this is the point where I will stop even trying to attempt using AI for anything.

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Two catchy weirdos, eighteen songs, and the hits just keep on coming in an exube…
entry.content: Two catchy weirdos, eighteen songs, and the hits just keep on coming in an exuberantly annoying show of creative superabundance. Their secret is that as unmediated pop postmodernists they can be themselves stealing from anywhere, modulating without strain or personal commitment from hick to nut to nerd. Like the cross-eyed bear in the regretful but not altogether kind "Hide Away Folk Family," their "shoes are laced with irony," but that doesn't doom them to art-school cleverness or never meaning
